patterns.name: Naive RAG
patterns.description: The foundational RAG pattern. Embeds documents into a vector database, retrieves top-K chunks by similarity, and feeds them to an LLM for generation. Fast to build, easy to understand.

patterns.name: Retrieve-and-Rerank RAG
patterns.description: Broadens initial retrieval (top-20) then applies a cross-encoder reranker to identify the most semantically relevant chunks before generation. Significantly improves precision over naive RAG.
patterns.example_stack: OpenAI embeddings + Pinecone + Cohere Rerank + GPT-4o

patterns.name: Hybrid RAG
patterns.tagline: Dense vector search + BM25 keyword search combined
patterns.description: Combines dense vector search (semantic) with sparse BM25 keyword search. Results are merged using reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) for superior coverage across both semantic and exact-match queries.
best_for: When recall matters as much as precision

patterns.name: Graph RAG
patterns.tagline: Knowledge graph + vector retrieval for relationship-aware answers
patterns.description: Builds a knowledge graph from documents alongside vector embeddings. Queries traverse graph relationships to find connected entities, enabling multi-hop reasoning that naive vector search cannot achieve.
patterns.team_needs: 2 ML engineers + 1 graph DB specialist + 1 backend developer

patterns.name: Agentic RAG (Router)
patterns.tagline: AI agent dynamically selects the best retrieval strategy per query
patterns.description: A routing agent analyzes each query and selects the best retrieval strategy: vector search, web search, SQL query, or API calls. Enables adaptive, intelligent retrieval across heterogeneous data sources.
best_for: When different questions need different strategies
weaknesses: Agent routing can fail or hallucinate

patterns.name: Multimodal RAG
patterns.tagline: Retrieve and reason over text, images, tables, and charts
patterns.description: Extends RAG to handle multimodal content. Uses vision-language models to embed and retrieve from images, charts, diagrams, and PDFs with visual elements alongside text.

patterns.name: Multi-Agent RAG
patterns.tagline: Orchestrator + specialized agents across multiple data sources
patterns.description: An orchestrator agent delegates tasks to specialized sub-agents, each responsible for different data sources (vector DB, web, CRM, Slack, etc.). Enables complex, parallelized information retrieval at enterprise scale.
best_for: Enterprise AI assistants spanning multiple systems
When data lives in many sources (Slack, CRM, docs, web)
High-value use cases justifying infrastructure investment
patterns.team_needs: 3–5 engineers including ML leads and platform engineers
patterns.example_stack: LangGraph + OpenAI + Multiple vector DBs + Tool integrations

patterns.name: Fine-tuning
patterns.tagline: Domain-adapted LLM — knowledge baked into weights, no retrieval
patterns.description: Fine-tune a base LLM on domain-specific data. The model learns domain knowledge through training rather than retrieval. Best when knowledge is stable, stylistic consistency matters, and volume is high.
weaknesses: Requires ML expertise and data curation
patterns.example_stack: OpenAI fine-tuning API or HuggingFace + custom training loop

patterns.name: RAG + Fine-tuning
patterns.tagline: Domain-adapted model with live retrieval — best of both worlds
patterns.description: Combines a domain fine-tuned LLM with a RAG retrieval pipeline. The fine-tuned model understands domain language and format while retrieval provides fresh, grounded, verifiable information.
best_for: When both knowledge and style must be tightly controlled
weaknesses: Two systems to maintain and evolve
patterns.example_stack: Custom fine-tuned Llama/GPT + OpenAI embeddings + Pinecone

patterns.name: Prompt Engineering
patterns.tagline: Optimized prompts with static context injection — no retrieval infrastructure
patterns.description: Uses carefully engineered prompts with static context, few-shot examples, and chain-of-thought techniques. Best for well-defined, bounded tasks where context fits in the model's context window.
patterns.example_stack: OpenAI GPT-4o + structured prompting + output parsing

patterns.name: Self-hosted / Air-gapped RAG
patterns.tagline: Fully on-premise RAG — no data leaves your infrastructure
patterns.description: Runs entirely on-premise or in a private cloud. Uses open-source embedding models and local LLMs (Llama, Mistral). Designed for organizations where no data can leave their own infrastructure.
weaknesses: Open-source models less capable than frontier LLMs
not_suitable_for: Cases where frontier model quality is critical

patterns.name: Conversational RAG
patterns.tagline: RAG with memory and multi-turn context management
patterns.description: Extends naive RAG with conversation memory. Maintains dialogue history, reformulates follow-up questions using prior context, and manages short-term vs long-term memory for coherent multi-turn interactions.
weaknesses: Memory storage adds latency and cost
History compression can lose important context
